Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾輩從大夫後，為士庶先 - Self-deprecating statement indicating a position between high-ranking officials and ordinary people.
  • 淡泊相期，志何取乎大快 - Reflects a philosophical stance of valuing simplicity over luxury.
  • 纷华奚事，情不用以过隆 - Suggests a rejection of excessive materialism and emotional excess.
  • 敢敬告我同僚，共守清规 - Formal and respectful way of giving advice.
  • 单刺可以通名，何烦全柬 - Preference for simplicity in communication.
  • 片词即能达意，岂必庄陈 - Emphasizes the efficiency of concise communication.
  • 至于宴会住还，惟期伸我积素 - Highlights the importance of maintaining old relationships.
  • 觥筹交错，止宜浃彼常情 - Suggests a moderate approach to social interactions.
  • 小酌不嫌于四两，屈量为佳 - Promotes moderation in drinking.
  • 大脔仅可以三斤，过饱不取 - Advocates for moderation in eating.
  • 非必为矫情之举，聊以表惜福之规 - Explains the rationale behind the moderate lifestyle.
  • 此约 - Concludes the document.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吾辈 - Refers to the group of people speaking, likely officials or scholars.
  • 大夫 - Refers to high-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 士庶 - Refers to commoners or ordinary people.
  • 淡泊 - Philosophical concept emphasizing simplicity and detachment from material desires.
  • 纷华 - Refers to luxury and extravagance.
  • 清规 - Refers to clear and simple rules or guidelines.
  • 单刺 - A simple card or note.
  • 全柬 - A full letter or formal correspondence.
  • 片词 - A few words or a brief message.
  • 庄陈 - Formal speech or presentation.
  • 觥筹交错 - Metaphor for social drinking and mingling.
  • 积素 - Long-standing friendship or relationship.
  • 大脔 - A large piece of meat.
  • 矫情 - Pretentious or artificial behavior.
  • 惜福 - Cherishing blessings or being grateful for what one has.

[170]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - Means 'the next day' or 'the following day.'
  • 得二鳖焉 - Means 'caught two turtles there.'
  • 径皆尺许 - Means 'their diameters were about a foot.'
  • 生乃悟曰 - Means 'the scholar then realized and said.'
  • 此曹、武余孽之深也 - Means 'This is the deep-rooted legacy of Cao and Wu.'
  • 其一书爵，其一书姓名 - Means 'one was inscribed with a title, and the other with a name.'
  • 至于今，犹颠倒磨折于鳞虫介羽之中，以大快天下万世之人心 - Means 'to this day, they are still being tormented in the realm of fish, insects, crustaceans, and birds, greatly pleasing the hearts of people for generations to come.'
  • 谁谓苍苍莽莽间漫无真宰也哉 - Means 'Who would say that the vast universe is devoid of a true ruler?'
  • 诛已往之奸回，愤余殃之厉气 - Means 'to punish past treachery and to vent the lingering evil influence.'
  • 阿瞒安在，武氏为谶 - Means 'Where is Cao Cao now, while the Wu clan is a foretold sign.'
  • 或为君而为臣，济恶皆同一辙；即成男而成女，厥罪亦可为均 - Means 'whether as a ruler or a minister, all who aid in evil follow the same path; whether male or female, their sins are equally grave.'
  • 炎鼎移来，继篡于王莽之后；中官乱始，倡淫于韦后之先 - Means 'the imperial power was transferred, following the usurpation of Wang Mang; the eunuchs caused chaos, leading to the debauchery of Empress Wei.'
  • 居然统魏妄尊，竟尔伪周僭号 - Means 'they arrogantly unified the state of Wei, falsely claiming respect; they eventually usurped the Zhou dynasty's name.'
  • 滥举孝廉之目，徒成才人之名 - Means 'they falsely claimed to be filial and upright, merely earning the reputation of talented individuals.'
  • 带剑入朝，汉相实为汉贼；垂帘听政，唐后即是唐妖 - Means 'carrying swords into the court, the Han prime minister was actually a traitor to the Han; ruling behind the curtain, the Tang empress was an evil figure in the Tang.'
  • 迹其欺妄之罪不殊也 - Means 'their crimes of deceit and falsehood are no different.'
  • 幽二帝于深宫，揽权自附；迁储君于远戍，窃柄为奸 - Means 'they imprisoned the two emperors in the inner palace, seizing power for themselves; they exiled the crown prince to a distant frontier, taking control through treachery.'
  • 献帝将啮指而降诏，掖门之衣带频看；高宗乃病目以临轩，内寝之声闻益厉 - Means 'Emperor Xian bit his finger to issue an edict, frequently looking at the door; Emperor Gaozong became blind to ascend the throne, with the sounds from the inner chambers growing louder.'
  • 挟天子而为令，汉廷之遗老被戕；窥神器以肆凶，唐室之诸宗几绝 - Means 'they held the emperor hostage to issue commands, causing the old scholars of the Han court to be killed; they coveted the imperial throne to commit atrocities, nearly exterminating the Tang imperial family.'
  • 炬北宫于八十万，犹夸元相阿衡；乱天纪于十三年，漫拟金轮天册 - Means 'they burned the Northern Palace with eighty thousand people, still boasting of Yuan Xian's role; they disrupted the heavenly order for thirteen years, rashly claiming the Golden Wheel and Heavenly Decree.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曹、武 - Refers to Cao Cao and the Wu clan, key figures in the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 王莽 - Refers to Wang Mang, a usurper who briefly established the Xin dynasty.
  • 韦后 - Refers to Empress Wei, a powerful and controversial figure in the Tang dynasty.
  • 孝廉 - Refers to a system of selecting officials based on moral and intellectual merit.
  • 汉相 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Han dynasty.
  • 唐后 - Refers to a female ruler in the Tang dynasty.
  • 炎鼎 - Refers to the imperial power or the imperial throne.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 文煨武爆 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous and can be interpreted as either 'gentle heating and intense bursts of heat' or 'a method of cooking that involves both gentle and intense heat.'
  • 三日而之上，六日复下之，转徒圆周 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous and can be interpreted as either 'move the eggs up and down in a circular motion over three and six days' or 'move the eggs in a circular motion over three and six days, alternating between up and down.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '望一望松林黄土，到处是荒冢麒麟做什么' is elliptical and requires interpretation to understand the speaker's intent.
  • The sentence '待说是明日出吊东郭氏，昨夜枕边没提掇' has a complex structure, requiring careful parsing to understand the relationship between the planned visit and the lack of communication.
  • The sentence '待说是东门祖帐饯行客，怎没个良朋折柳坝桥河' is a rhetorical question, questioning the absence of a traditional farewell scene.
  • The sentence '又只见几家坟上哭声哀，摆开一抬大祭盒' describes a scene of mourning, with the juxtaposition of crying and the large offering box.
  • The sentence '这齐人一见喜的旋风转，来了他五脏庙里救命佛' describes a sudden change in mood, with the metaphor of a savior.
  • The sentence '大叉步直到纸蝴蝶灰飞去，只见他咕咚倒地半截矬' describes a forceful movement followed by a sudden collapse.
  • The sentence '那些人看不上他那花子相，给了他一壶奠酒两个供馍' describes a negative reaction to someone's appearance, followed by an offering.
  • The sentence '这人饿狗抢食尽着吃，却不道气杀听风俊俏婆' describes a person eating voraciously, followed by a dramatic reaction.
  • The sentence '他这里扑簌簌泪珠儿不住的掉，他那里刮搭着嘴皮还四下里睃' describes a scene of deep sorrow and suspicion.
  • The sentence '天杀的实指望华堂开宴吃大酒，谁知道乱葬岗头扳剩馍' contrasts a grand banquet with a desolate burial ground, highlighting the disparity between expectations and reality.
  • The sentence '我悔恨当初不该来看这看，到弄的进退两难无奈何' expresses regret and a state of indecision.
  • The sentence '这齐妇跢了跢金莲回去罢，他还在坟子旁里嚼着骨头就酒掉' describes a graceful departure and a crude behavior at a grave.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孟夫子 - Respectful title for Mencius, a Confucian philosopher.
  • 商鞅 - Shang Yang, a reformer of the Qin state during the Warring States period.
  • 范雎 - Fan Ju, a statesman of the state of Qin.
  • 张仪 - Zhang Yi, a diplomat of the state of Qin.
  • 齐人家里 - Reference to the state of Qi, often used in classical texts to denote a place of refuge or support.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂取土塞其孔 - Literal phrase meaning 'then took earth to plug the hole,' describing a physical action.
  • 持凿往 - Means 'carried a chisel to go,' indicating the preparation for a task.
  • 将洞开而指其藏 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to open a hole and point to the treasure,' suggesting an intention to reveal or access something valuable.
  • 至则迷其处 - Means 'when he arrived, he was lost in the place,' indicating confusion upon arrival.
  • 但闻崖内人呼 - Means 'but heard people inside the cliff calling,' describing an auditory experience.
  • 宝藏库后垣有漏隙 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'there is a leak in the wall of the treasure house,' suggesting a vulnerability or flaw.
  • 去十八钱，觅工补之 - Means 'spend eighteen coins, find a worker to fix it,' indicating a cost and a solution.
  • 其声欲震陵谷 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'the sound was about to shake mountains and valleys,' emphasizing the intensity of the noise.
  • 樵者惊怖返 - Means 'the woodcutter was terrified and returned,' describing the woodcutter's reaction.
  • 有此一处小洞天 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'there is a small paradise here,' suggesting a hidden, secluded place.
  • 更是多宝崖 - Means 'it is even a treasure cliff,' emphasizing the value of the place.
  • 安得到彼 - Means 'how can one reach there,' expressing difficulty or impossibility.
  • 掉臂游行其中 - Means 'waving arms to stroll freely in it,' suggesting ease and freedom.
  • 一开眼界 - Means 'to open one's eyes,' indicating a new perspective or realization.
  • 若到宝山 - Means 'if one reaches the treasure mountain,' suggesting a valuable opportunity.
  • 那有空回 - Means 'there would be no empty return,' indicating that one would gain something.
  • 奈何无问津者 - Means 'why is there no one asking the way,' expressing disappointment or frustration.
  • 熟卵石 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'ripe egg stones,' suggesting something valuable or precious.
  • 高十余寻 - Means 'tall over ten *xun*,' indicating the height of the tree.
  • 可半亩园 - Means 'about half an acre garden,' describing the size of the area.
  • 有鹳巢数处 - Means 'there are several stork nests,' describing the presence of storks.
  • 林子幼稚好戏 - Means 'Lin's son, young and fond of play,' describing the character of the son.
  • 尝猿步能至树巅 - Means 'once climbed like a monkey to the top of the tree,' describing a physical feat.
  • 见巢内二卵如柚 - Means 'saw two eggs inside the nest as big as a pomelo,' describing the size of the eggs.
  • 携而下 - Means 'carried them down,' describing the action.
  • 入釜燃薪 - Means 'put them in a pot and lit firewood,' describing the preparation for cooking.
  • 以待剖食 - Means 'to wait to cut and eat,' describing the intention.
  • 其母瞥见 - Means 'his mother glanced and saw,' describing the mother's reaction.
  • 此异物 - Means 'this strange thing,' describing the eggs.
  • 不可以啖 - Means 'cannot be eaten,' indicating a prohibition.
  • 且鹳巢吾家 - Means 'and the stork nest is in our home,' indicating ownership.
  • 盖亦有年 - Means 'has been there for many years,' indicating the duration.
  • 乃令其子仍归诸巢 - Means 'then ordered his son to return them to the nest,' describing the mother's instruction.
  • 阅数月而树间啾啾 - Means 'after several months, there were chirping sounds in the trees,' describing a change over time.
  • 又数日而小鹳立枝头学飞 - Means 'after several more days, small storks stood on branches to learn to fly,' describing the growth and development.
  • 既而随老鹳翔于云表 - Means 'then followed the old storks to soar in the clouds,' describing the final stage of development.
  • 林子惑焉 - Means 'Lin's son was puzzled,' describing the son's confusion.
  • 复缘上以觇其异 - Means 'again climbed up to observe the strange phenomenon,' describing the son's curiosity.
  • 巢间余粪败草中 - Means 'in the nest's leftover feces and rotten grass,' describing the environment.
  • 贮一黑珠盈掬 - Means 'stored a black pearl that could be held in one's palm,' describing the discovery.
  • 非金非石 - Means 'neither metal nor stone,' describing the nature of the pearl.
  • 黝光可鉴 - Means 'with a dark luster that could be used as a mirror,' describing the appearance of the pearl.
  • 怀归藏之药笼 - Means 'carried it back and stored it in the medicine box,' describing the action.
  • 久而尝陈几端 - Means 'after a long time, it was placed on the table,' describing the final placement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 粤香山小揽卖药林氏家 - Refers to a specific location and family in Guangdong, providing a cultural and geographical context.
  • 鹳巢 - Storks are often associated with good luck and longevity in Chinese culture.
  • 熟卵石 - The metaphor of 'ripe egg stones' suggests something valuable or precious, possibly alluding to the idea of hidden treasures.
  • 药笼 - A medicine box is a common household item in traditional Chinese medicine, indicating the family's profession or interest in medicine.

Proper Nouns

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • Foreign merchants were common in ancient China, often involved in trade along the Silk Road.

  • The southern region, possibly in Indochina, was known for its rulers and trade in precious stones.
